channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iceberg lettuce at Taco Bell linked to cyclospora outbreak in 5 states
item.description: Federal health officials have identified a single supplier of the produce from Mexico that was served in Taco Bell restaurants in five states.
item.content:encoded:
Federal health officials have identified a single supplier of the produce from Mexico that was served in Taco Bell restaurants in five states.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: The U.S.-Iran battle over the Strait of Hormuz raises risks for global waterways
item.description: Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.
item.content:encoded:
Recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz could set a dangerous precedent, with other countries attempting to claim important waterways, analysts say.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Wildfire smoke is like smoking 'half a pack a day.' Here's how to protect yourself
item.description: As Canadian wildfires spread smoke across the U.S. the air pollution is dangerous to health. But there are ways to protect yourself. Here's what to know.
item.content:encoded:
As Canadian wildfires spread smoke across the U.S. the air pollution is dangerous to health. But there are ways to protect yourself. Here's what to know.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda

item.title: Is smoke in your home? Here's how to make an air purifier using a box fan
item.description: Smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is sending air quality indexes to record levels, posing a hazard for millions of people. Here are tips for how to breathe easier in your home.
item.content:encoded:
Smoke from wildfires in Canada and Minnesota is sending air quality indexes to record levels, posing a hazard for millions of people. Here are tips for how to breathe easier in your home.

(Image credit: Finn Gomez)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Pakistani forces kill 24 militants in border raids near Afghanistan
item.description: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.
item.content:encoded:
The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants over the previous 24 hours. The operations were in response to attacks by militants earlier in the week.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's primetime speech. And, at least 2 dead in major Texas flooding
item.description: President Trump gave a primetime speech raising claims of voting vulnerabilities but offered no new evidence. And, at least 2 people have died in major flooding in Texas.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump gave a primetime speech raising claims of voting vulnerabilities but offered no new evidence. And, at least 2 people have died in major flooding in Texas.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Photos: When the World Cup came to town
item.description: NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.
item.content:encoded:
NPR member station photographers captured images of World Cup watch parties outside stadium gates, on street corners and in public parks and squares as the World Cup became, for a brief period, a part of local life.

(Image credit: Patricia Lim)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: U.S. strikes bridges in Iran; Tehran targets U.S. bases in the Gulf
item.description: The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran expanded their targets in the latest round of strikes on Friday, as fighting over the control of the Strait of Hormuz reignites fears of an all-out war.

(Image credit: AFP)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: A bug commonly sold at pet stores is helping scientists with a vexing task
item.description: The larvae of a beetle native to South and Central America, the critters are the perfect solution to sticky problem: How to prepare an animal skeleton for scientific use.
item.content:encoded:
The larvae of a beetle native to South and Central America, the critters are the perfect solution to sticky problem: How to prepare an animal skeleton for scientific use.

(Image credit: MirekKijewski/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Trump's team says 'no children' died from USAID cuts. Consider these 3 cases
item.description: Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.
item.content:encoded:
Both Marco Rubio and Elon Musk, who led the effort to sunset the foreign aid agency, have said that no deaths have been linked to the cutting of its funding. These parents tell a different story.

(Image credit: From left: David Augustine, Lameck Nyagudi and Kazeem Olawale Nasiru for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
